title: Killing of Putana and Salvation of other Demons

analysis.core_story_summary: When Vasudev was released from the prison, Nand visited Mathura on some official trip. He also came to see Vasudev. Vasudev greeted him for the birth of a son and instructed him to return to Gokul as soon as possible.

audience_notes.children_8_12: Use simple language, explain divine names as characters in a sacred legend.
audience_notes.teens_13_17: Connect events to decision-making, identity, and consequences.
audience_notes.young_adults_18_30: Highlight ethics, leadership, and social relevance.
audience_notes.devotional_traditional: Preserve sacred framing and theological terms respectfully.
audience_notes.global_general: Keep cultural context brief and avoid insider assumptions.

sensitivity_notes: Contains conflict imagery that may need age-appropriate framing.
Includes intense mythic elements (curses, demons, destruction).
References family and marriage roles rooted in traditional contexts.

redesigned_story_by_demographic.children_8_12: **Killing of Putana and Salvation of other Demons — Kids (8-12):** When Vasudev was released from the prison, Nand visited Mathura on some official trip. He also came to see Vasudev. Vasudev greeted him for the birth of a son and instructed him to return to Gokul as soon as possible. This story teaches devotion and disciplined practice, dedication and sacrifice, divine grace, truthfulness. Think of it like a legend that helps us choose kindness, courage, and respect.
redesigned_story_by_demographic.teens_13_17: **Killing of Putana and Salvation of other Demons — Teens (13-17):** When Vasudev was released from the prison, Nand visited Mathura on some official trip. He also came to see Vasudev. Vasudev greeted him for the birth of a son and instructed him to return to Gokul as soon as possible. For teens, the key idea is devotion and disciplined practice, dedication and sacrifice, divine grace, truthfulness. It shows how choices, discipline, and character shape outcomes.

He also came to see Vasudev. Vasudev greeted him for the birth of a son and instructed him to return to Gokul as soon as possible. A modern takeaway is devotion and disciplined practice, dedication and sacrifice, divine grace, truthfulness. The narrative can be read as a reflection on purpose, responsibility, and values-driven action.

The episode highlights devotion and disciplined practice, dedication and sacrifice, divine grace, truthfulness. redesigned_story_by_demographic.global_general: **Killing of Putana and Salvation of other Demons — Global General Audience:** When Vasudev was released from the prison, Nand visited Mathura on some official trip. In universal terms, it explores devotion and disciplined practice, dedication and sacrifice, divine grace, truthfulness.
